posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Talita Alencar vs. Julia Polastri
Julia Polastri via Decision
I’m not uber confident in this one for obvious WMMA reasons but Polastri is younger and bigger than Alencar who I think is a bit too one dimensional. Polastri just needs to keep this standing and avoid the clinch and she should take the decision victory.

Max Griffin vs. Victor Valenzuela
Victor Valenzuela via Decision
Max Griffin’s 40? Damn, well this one probably won’t go his way either. Valenzuela is one of the rare “Lose your Contender Series bout but get signed anyway” guys so I don’t have much as far as expectations for him. I don’t see him finishing Griffin who’s still tough but has seen better days. The newcomer takes the Decision.

Francis Marshall vs. Lucas Brennan
Francis Marshall via Decision
This isn’t going to be close because Marshall is going to dog walk Brennan from pillar to post. There’s something in the air tonight and it’s Decision wins.

Jafel Filho vs. Cody Durden
Jafel Filho via 1st Round Submission
Fighter Removed: Cody Durden. Enough.

Mayra Bueno Silva vs. Michelle Montague
Michelle Montague via Decision
Fighter Removed: Mayra Bueno Silva. Congrats on the new kid tho. Enjoy the family life.

Sedriques Dumas vs. Jackson McVey
Sedriques Dumas via Decision
****** meme fight that hopefully delivers on entertainment. I can’t pick the Alice In Chains roadie and Sedriques is so **** but atleast he’s athletic enough to compensate for his ****tiness. Just get this one over with as soon as possible. Dumbass take the Decision.

Rodolfo Vieira vs. Eric McConico
Rodolfo Vieira via 1st Round Submission
McConico isn’t good whatsoever and it’s a matter of when and not if when it comes to Rodolfo getting the finish. I have little faith in McConico’s grappling defense so I’ll take Vieira to get the early Sub.

Marcus Buchecha vs. Ryan Spann
Marcus Buchecha via 2nd Round Submission
The rare back-to-back submission finishes on a bleh card to spice things up. We’re doing the Fortis Fade because I also don’t trust Spann’s grappling defense especially if he gets gassed hunting for a finish and fails early. Buchecha finishes him with a 2nd round Kimura.

Montel Jackson vs. Raoni Barcelos
Montel Jackson via Decision
Montel knows he needs to redeem himself after some lackluster performances but Barcelos is on a tear right now in his late 30’s. If Montel can work behind his jab and kicks he can keep Barcelos at bay in the striking exchanges. He’s also my dawg but this is a fight he needs if he wants to keep his slot near the Top 10 in the rankings so I’ll take him to get the hard fought decision nod.

Davey Grant vs. Adrian Luna Martinetti
Davey Grant via Decision
You can’t teach an old dog new tricks but Davey is a battle tested pitbull who’s still able to bring it. Martinetti is like the rest of last year’s DWCS contract winners, you gotta prove it to me first before I’m a believer. I know what I’m getting in Davey and he’s going to teach the newbie there’s level to this and wins a decision that could be FOTN.

Rafa Garcia vs. Alexander Hernandez
Alexander Hernandez via 1st Round TKO
Finally, somebody’s getting knocked out in this one. Hernandez has revitalized his career and is much more dangerous on the feet than Rafa. First round head kick TKO for El Gran Chango.

Norma Durmont vs. Joselyne Edwards
Norma Durmont via Decision
And we’re back to 15 minute fights and this fight does nothing for me. Never been high on Edwards and Norma is a contender (I guess) at 135 so I’ll take the better fighter to outclass Edwards for the distance.

Aljamain Sterling vs. Youssef Zalal
Aljamain Sterling via Decision
So glad this card is over with. Zalal’s hot streak comes to a halt as Aljo is just more experienced in 5 round fights and looks more comfortable at 145 than I would have expected. Zalal really needs to stick-n-move one the outside but he’ll only be able to do that for so long until Aljo will be able to control him and he just won’t be able to fend off the takedowns and pressure. Aljo takes the…Decision and that’s all folks.
